slides.title:
Home Physical
Therapy Platform
slides.description: WizeCare brings physical therapy into the patient's home. The clinical tools were solid — but the platform was too complex for busy clinicians to adopt in real practice.

slides.label: The Context
slides.title: Clinicians who don't have time to learn new software
slides.content: Physiotherapists using WizeCare are already managing packed clinic days. They rely on manual routines built over years — and are quick to abandon tools that add steps instead of removing them.
slides.highlight: For WizeCare to succeed, the system needed to feel like a time-saver — not an extra task.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Three places the experience broke down
slides.description: We visited physiotherapy clinics, ran user interviews, and reviewed support tickets. The friction consistently appeared in the same three places.

issues.title: Patient screen unclear
issues.description: Clinicians couldn't tell where to find care plans or patient details — oversized tabs and no clear hierarchy made navigation unpredictable

issues.title: Care plan editing too complex
issues.description: Creating or editing a care plan required navigating multiple screens, causing confusion and context loss along the way

issues.title: No protocol creation flow
issues.description: Physiotherapists couldn't build protocols from scratch — the system forced them into rigid predefined templates that didn't fit complex cases

slides.label: User Research
slides.title: What clinicians told us
slides.content: We visited several physiotherapy clinics to understand the challenges firsthand. The same frustrations surfaced everywhere.

quotes.text: I spend more time navigating than actually managing my patients.
quotes.author: Physiotherapist, Clinic A

quotes.text: I never know which screen has what I need.
quotes.author: Physiotherapist, Clinic B

quotes.text: Editing a care plan takes so many steps — I avoid it when I can.
quotes.author: Physiotherapist, Clinic C

quotes.text: I still use paper notes because they're faster.
quotes.author: Physiotherapist, Clinic D

slides.label: Design Goals
slides.title: Three friction points. Three things to fix.

goals.title: Simplify navigation
goals.description: Make patient data, care plans, and key actions reachable without hunting.

goals.title: Streamline care plan editing
goals.description: Consolidate a multi-screen process into one focused, coherent flow.

goals.title: Enable protocol creation from scratch
goals.description: Give experienced clinicians the flexibility to build and customise their own protocols.

goals.title: Reduce the learning curve
goals.description: Design should be self-explanatory — for both new and experienced users.

slides.title: Patient Management
slides.subtitle: The first place clinicians land — and where they spent most of their time feeling lost.

slides.label: Patient Screen
slides.title: Everything a clinician needs — in one place
slides.beforeDescription: The patient page was cluttered and unclear — oversized tabs, no visual hierarchy, and adherence metrics buried where no one could find them.
slides.afterDescription: A dedicated patient page with clear separation between info, plans, and progress — and adherence metrics surfaced as the primary clinical signal.
beforeBullets: Oversized tabs dominated the screen with no clear hierarchy
No dedicated patient page — clinicians unsure how to view plans
Adherence metrics buried — hardest to find, most clinically useful
afterBullets: Dedicated patient page centralises all information
Clear separation between patient info, active plans, and progress
Adherence metrics prominent — visible without navigating away
slides.highlight: Clinicians went from searching across screens to finding everything in one predictable place.

slides.title: Care Plan Editing
slides.subtitle: The most frequent task in the platform — and the one with the most unnecessary steps.

slides.label: Care Plan Editing
slides.title: Editing a plan shouldn't require a map
slides.beforeDescription: The old flow was spread across multiple screens with no clear separation between exercises — lengthy, cluttered, and easy to get lost in.
slides.afterDescription: Everything consolidated onto one screen. Each exercise on its own card. Inline editing throughout.
beforeBullets: Multiple screens to complete a single edit — context lost every step
Exercises in dense, unseparated lists — hard to manage at scale
No patient health data visible while editing
afterBullets: Single focused screen for the full editing flow
Each exercise displayed as its own card — easy to compare and reorder
Patient health data pinned throughout for constant clinical context
slides.highlight: Irrelevant information removed entirely — the screen shows only what clinicians need to act.

slides.title: Protocol Builder
slides.subtitle: Experienced physiotherapists want to build their own protocols — the old system gave them no way to do it.

slides.label: Research Insight
slides.quote: Physiotherapists prefer to create their own protocols rather than rely on predefined care plans. Each case requires specific adjustments — rigid templates just don't fit how they work.
slides.author: Clinic interviews

slides.label: Protocol Builder
slides.title: From rigid templates to full clinical control
slides.beforeDescription: No defined protocol creation workflow. Expert clinicians were forced into multi-step navigation with no way to build from scratch.
slides.afterDescription: A drag-and-drop Kanban builder modelled on the care plan editor — familiar from day one, powerful enough for complex cases.
beforeBullets: No protocol creation flow — experts had no real starting point
Multi-step navigation with no clear thread
Rigid templates didn't fit complex or custom cases
afterBullets: Dedicated protocol builder with drag-and-drop Kanban layout
Exercise library on the left, treatment board on the right
Same card system as care plan editor — zero additional learning
slides.highlight: Reusing the card system across both flows meant the new builder felt immediately familiar.

slides.label: Outcomes
slides.title: Less friction. More time for patients.

outcomes.title: 10–15 min saved per patient
outcomes.description: Streamlined workflows meant less time navigating, more time on clinical decisions.

outcomes.title: Fewer errors
outcomes.description: Clearer layouts and inline editing reduced misclicks and prescription mistakes.

outcomes.title: Daily usage increased
outcomes.description: Clinicians who had avoided the platform started using it as their primary tool.

outcomes.title: Support requests dropped
outcomes.description: A self-explanatory system meant less dependence on onboarding and training.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Designing systems that respect the people who use them.
